mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle recovery periods with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction with Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales signal synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and RSAFS uptrend fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with backtest accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling inflation indicates weak demand pulling through the supply chain, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL declines during late-cycle periods.
